title: Rosa × dumetorum amblyphylla
taxonomy: Rosa × dumetorum amblyphylla is a hybrid rose species, classified as a subspecies of the Rosa dumetorum. It belongs to the family Rosaceae and is native to parts of Europe and Asia. The classification of Rosa × dumetorum amblyphylla is still being researched and confirmed by botanists. Its taxonomic classification is considered complex due to its hybrid nature. Rosa × dumetorum amblyphylla is often classified under the section Caninae within the genus Rosa.
uses: Rosa × dumetorum amblyphylla is a rose species with limited information available. However, it's worth noting that various rose species have been traditionally used in herbal medicine. Rosa × dumetorum amblyphylla may be used as a general tonic, promoting overall health and well-being due to its potential antioxidant properties. Additionally, rose species have been used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation. Further research is needed to confirm the specific medicinal uses of Rosa × dumetorum amblyphylla.
benefits: Rosa × dumetorum amblyphylla, also known as the spiny rose or rose of bramble, has been found to have various potential health benefits. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer.
